behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at real Git subprocess behavior plus parser/classifier handoff. Rename detection comes from real Git diff/name-status semantics; mocks can hide cases where Git emits rename records differently or not at all. Behavior text is explicitly about repository change categorization, not a single pure function.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp Git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows one rename and zero paired add/delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real Git subprocess output/encoding and binary diff handling. In-process fakes often miss real byte/encoding/exit-code behavior that causes crashes. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real repo with a committed binary file change, run the CLI process, verify non-zero crash does not occur, and assert binary file is included in the relevant count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Real failure commonly comes from Git command behavior in unborn HEAD repos (real exit code/stderr semantics). Mocking Git responses can hide this boundary condition.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real Git repo with no commits, execute CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual Git history, requiring real repository state and Git command outputs. Integration-only fixtures risk drifting from real Git history semantics and can miss command-level mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and compare emitted counts to expected counts derived from that known history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Requires real Git index vs working tree state transitions and CLI/Git invocation behavior. This boundary is OS+subprocess+repo-state dependent; mocks can hide index/worktree discrepancies.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent file changes once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI in both states, and assert normalized structured stdout is identical.
